message: The other day, I found myself in a bit of a pickle when our neighborhood experienced a sudden power outage. I had been meaning to put together a go-bag, but life kept getting in the way. Luckily, I had a few essentials stashed in my kitchen drawer: some flashlights, a couple of bottled waters, and even a battery-operated radio I had forgotten about. It felt like a small win to have those bits on hand as the kids and I huddled together, playing games by flashlight and listening to updates on the radio. It reminded me that even little steps can make a difference when unexpected moments arise. Have you had any small wins in your own preparedness journey lately?
